comment_text.sn9.7:1.1: This Nāgadatta is not mentioned elsewhere. His names suggests his parents had made an offering to dragon-spirits for a child, thus he is “Nāga-given”; cf. Devadatta, Brahmadatta, etc.
comment_text.sn9.7:2.1: For this verse, follow readings in Bodhi, Connected Discourses.

root_text.sn9.7:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā nāgadatto kosalesu viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.sn9.7:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā nāgadatto atikālena gāmaṁ pavisati, atidivā paṭikkamati.
root_text.sn9.7:1.3: Atha kho yā tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe adhivatthā devatā āyasmato nāgadattassa anukampikā atthakāmā āyasmantaṁ nāgadattaṁ saṁvejetukāmā yenāyasmā nāgadatto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ nāgadattaṁ gāthāhi ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn9.7:4.1: Atha kho āyasmā nāgadatto tāya devatāya saṁvejito saṁvegamāpādīti.

translation_text.sn9.7:1.1: At one time Venerable Nāgadatta was staying in the land of the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
translation_text.sn9.7:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Nāgadatta had been entering the village too early and returning late in the day.
translation_text.sn9.7:1.3: Then the deity haunting that forest had sympathy for Nāgadatta, wanting what’s best for him. So they approached him wanting to stir him up, and recited these verses:
translation_text.sn9.7:2.2: and returning too late in the day,
translation_text.sn9.7:3.1: I’m afraid for Nāgadatta; he’s reckless,
translation_text.sn9.7:3.3: May he not come under the King of Death’s power,
translation_text.sn9.7:3.4: under the sway of the terminator!”
translation_text.sn9.7:4.1: Impelled by that deity, Venerable Nāgadatta was struck with a sense of urgency.

variant_text.sn9.7:3.4: vasaṁ upesī”ti → vasamesīti (bj, sya-all, km); vasam eyyā ti (pts1ed, pts2ed)
